qa_pairs.question: Why is it necessary to renew Alliance accounts annually?
qa_pairs.answer: Annual renewal of all user accounts is essential to collect up-to-date information required by our granting agencies and to deactivate accounts that are no longer in use.

qa_pairs.question: Who is required to renew their Alliance account and when?
qa_pairs.answer: All Alliance users must renew their account between May 6 and June 9, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if an Alliance account is not renewed by the deadline?
qa_pairs.answer: If you do not renew your Alliance account by June 9, 2026, your account will be automatically deactivated, meaning you will not be able to access your data, though it will not be deleted immediately.

qa_pairs.question: Can a deactivated Alliance account be reactivated?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you can renew your account at any time after it has been deactivated.

qa_pairs.question: Are new Alliance account holders required to renew their accounts?
qa_pairs.answer: An exception is made for recent account holders: if you created your account after January 1, you do not have to renew it at this time. However, you still have to accept the new policies and consents, which will prompt on CCDB the first time you try to log in.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the Privacy Policy and other agreements I've accepted for my Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: All agreements you are required to accept or indicate your preference for on CCDB can be seen in the [https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/agreements/user_index Agreements] page once signed. The policies are also available on the Alliance [https://alliancecan.ca/en/policies website].

qa_pairs.question: What types of agreements and consents are part of the Alliance account process?
qa_pairs.answer: The agreements include Policies, Standards, Terms, and Procedures; Consent to Access User Data; Optional transfer of file ownership on account deactivation; and Consent to receive optional news and communications from the Digital Research Alliance of Canada.

qa_pairs.question: How is a primary role defined for an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: Your primary role must correspond to your main position, generally the one you will hold for the longest. For example, a full-time faculty appointment at one institution or the role with your primary supervisor if you are a student or researcher.

qa_pairs.question: What is the importance of a primary role in an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: Primary roles are important because they help us to manage accounts with multiple roles, facilitate communication with those users, and assist with reporting.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if a user's primary role is not renewed during the annual renewal period?
qa_pairs.answer: If you do not renew your primary role during the annual renewal period, your account will be deactivated.

qa_pairs.question: Can I change my primary role in my Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes. Users with multiple active roles can change their primary role at any time on CCDB's ''Home'' page by going to the role of your choosing and clicking on the ''Make this role primary'' checkbox next to it.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my role or situation has changed (e.g., moving to a new faculty position or changing academic programs)?
qa_pairs.answer: If your role has changed, you must apply for a new role. You can do this by logging into CCDB and going to ''My Account'' → ''Apply for a new role''.

qa_pairs.question: How do I manage multiple roles if I need them for my Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: If you need several roles, please request one role at a time. Any new role approved is set as your primary role by default, but you can uncheck the ''Make this role primary?'' box if you don't wish it to be. To deactivate old roles, click on “disable old roles.”

qa_pairs.question: My Alliance account has expired and is deactivated, but I still need to access my data to move it off the Alliance Federation clusters. What should I do?
qa_pairs.answer: Please send an email to support@tech.alliancecan.ca so that we can assist you with your request.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I have an account renewal question not addressed in the FAQ?
qa_pairs.answer: Please send an email to renewals@tech.alliancecan.ca for assistance with your account renewal question.

qa_pairs.question: What are the consequences for a faculty member if they do not renew their Alliance account by the deadline?
qa_pairs.answer: If a faculty member does not renew their active account between May 6 and June 9, 2026, their account will be automatically deactivated. This will also deactivate the accounts of all sponsored users, pause any submitted jobs, and cause their research group to lose access to files until the sponsor account is reactivated.

qa_pairs.question: Can a faculty member reactivate their account after it has been deactivated?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, faculty accounts can be reactivated at any time, even after they have been deactivated.
